name: arch_inv_allows_zero_layers
code:
proof fn phi_1_arch_inv_allows_zero_layers(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
    ensures
        arch.layers.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: If provable, inv() is vacuously satisfied by empty architectures, making the invariant too weak to be meaningful.

name: entry_base_zero_index_differs_from_base
code:
proof fn phi_2_entry_base_zero_index_differs_from_base(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, 0) != base,
{
}
reason: Entry base at index 0 must equal the base address; a mismatch breaks the fundamental address decomposition.

name: well_formed_allows_zero_entries
code:
proof fn phi_3_well_formed_allows_zero_entries(d: Directory)
    requires
        d.well_formed(),
    ensures
        d.entries.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: If provable, well-formed directories can be empty, yielding page table levels with no mappable regions.

name: page_frame_size_zero_under_inv
code:
proof fn phi_4_page_frame_size_zero_under_inv(d: Directory, i: nat)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        i < d.entries.len(),
        d.entries[i as int] is Page,
    ensures
        d.entries[i as int]->Page_0.frame.size == 0,
{
}
reason: If provable, the invariant permits zero-size page frames, which represent physically meaningless memory regions.

name: subdir_base_equals_parent_base
code:
proof fn phi_5_subdir_base_equals_parent_base(d: Directory, i: nat)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        0 < i,
        i < d.entries.len(),
        d.entries[i as int] is Directory,
    ensures
        d.entries[i as int]->Directory_0.base_vaddr == d.base_vaddr,
{
}
reason: If provable, non-first subdirectories share the parent's base address, causing overlapping virtual address ranges.
